title: Entergy Texas declares quarterly dividend on preferred stock
author: Prnewswire
description: Entergy Texas, Inc. has declared a quarterly dividend payment of $0.3359375 per share on its Series A Preferred Stock, payable on July 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of July 2, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of Entergy Texas, the subsidiary's ability to declare dividends reflects the overall financial health and operational stability of the Entergy Corporation group.

The company is maintaining its dividend payments to preferred shareholders, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to returning value to investors. Regular dividend declarations are typically viewed positively by the market.

title: Entergy (ETR) Stock Is Trending After Hours As CEO Tells Jim Cramer Data Centers Won't Burden Ratepayers: Here's What He Said
author: Mohd Haider
description: Entergy CEO Drew Marsh told CNBC's Jim Cramer that the company's 'Fair Share Plus' framework ensures data center operators cover all incremental infrastructure costs and some fixed costs, protecting residential ratepayers. The framework is expected to generate approximately $7 billion in savings for existing customers over 15-20 year contracts. Marsh emphasized that data centers want to be good neighbors and protect their reputations in communities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CEO's reassurance about protecting ratepayers from data center burden through the 'Fair Share Plus' framework, expected $7 billion in customer savings, stock gained 33.39% over 12 months, Barclays maintains Overweight rating, and positive technical indicators with Growth score of 82.40 suggest investor confidence and upward momentum.

title: Financial Advisor Exits Position in Industrial Stock, According to Latest SEC Filing
author: Jake Lerch
description: Aurora Investment Counsel sold its entire 58,061-share stake in Gorman-Rupp (GRC) during Q1 2026, valued at approximately $3.43 million. The stock has surged 137% over two years due to AI data center demand for cooling systems, but now trades at a P/E ratio of 35x, above both market and historical averages, raising valuation concerns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the company has demonstrated strong performance (137% growth over two years) driven by AI data center demand for cooling systems, the exit by Aurora Investment Counsel and elevated valuation metrics (P/E of 35x vs. 32x market average and 31x historical average) suggest potential overvaluation concerns, creating a mixed outlook.
Mentioned as a top holding of Aurora Investment Counsel ($3.27 million, 1.8% of AUM) with no specific news or sentiment drivers in the article.

title: Meta's $27 Billion Data Center Places the Spotlight on Natural Gas Stocks
author: Jack Delaney
description: Meta's $27 billion Louisiana data center investment is driving demand for natural gas and energy infrastructure. The company will fund seven new natural gas power plants and battery storage through a deal with Entergy. As AI workloads strain traditional grids, energy demand from data centers is projected to increase 50% by 2027 and 165% by 2030, benefiting natural gas and energy companies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Direct beneficiary of Meta's $27 billion data center deal in Louisiana; subsidiary secured contract for seven new natural gas power plants and collaborative nuclear development framework; shares up 25% year-to-date
Major capital investment in data center infrastructure; positive for energy sector but article focuses on energy company benefits rather than Meta's prospects

title: The Clock May Be Ticking on Energy Transfer Stock Trading Under $20 -- Should You Buy Now?
author: Jack Delaney
description: Energy Transfer (ET), a major midstream company with 140,000 miles of pipeline infrastructure, is positioned to benefit from rapidly expanding data center demand and rising natural gas needs. With existing deals including Oracle and Entergy Louisiana, and a forward P/E ratio of 11.3, the stock trading under $20 may not stay there long if the company demonstrates continued momentum in upcoming earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong positioning in growing data center market, existing major contracts (Oracle, Entergy), fair valuation with 11.3 forward P/E, attractive 7% dividend yield, and low volatility. Stock up 13% in 2026 with potential for further upside.
